content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion (spelled the British English way) is taught as a metacognitive skill for high school students to reflect on their own writing to spot gaps and improve clarity, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured language learning framework of reflexion as a classroom writing exercise to ground their abstract self-correction processes. Framing LLM self-reflection as a iterative 'writing workshop' where the model acts as both student and instructor reviewing its own outputs creates a more intuitive, structured self-correction workflow than current contrastive perspective methods.
